events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Mick Jagger, the iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, turns 83 on July 26, 2026.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Longevity
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still strutting! Mick Jagger continues to defy age with his legendary stage presence and timeless hits. What's your favourite Stones track that still gets you moving?

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television, MTV, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first music video.

angles.name: The Video Revolution
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV changed music forever, kicking off with The Buggles' prophetic 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Did it really kill radio, or just make it better?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' anniversary
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album, 'Definitely Maybe,' on August 29, 1994.

angles.name: Britpop's Big Bang
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of British music. From 'Supersonic' to 'Live Forever,' what's your standout track from this Britpop masterpiece?

events.title: Woodstock Festival anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair took place from August 15-18, 1969, featuring iconic performances over three days of peace and music.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people descended on a farm in New York for Woodstock. Which legendary performance from '3 Days of Peace & Music' do you wish you'd seen live?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: August 16, 2026, marks 49 years since the passing of Elvis Presley, who would have been 91.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Forty-nine years today, the world lost a legend. Elvis Presley's impact on music is immeasurable. What's your favourite song from The King that still resonates with you?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur shooting
events.blurb: On September 7, 1996, Tupac Shakur was shot in a drive-by incident in Las Vegas, tragically passing away six days later. He would have been 55.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Thirty years ago, the music world was rocked by the shooting of Tupac Shakur. His legacy lives on through his powerful lyrics and undeniable influence. What's your go-to Tupac track?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: September 18, 2026, marks 56 years since the passing of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84.

angles.name: Guitar God Remembered
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we lost the incomparable Jimi Hendrix. His innovative guitar work redefined rock music. What's your favourite Hendrix riff or song?

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic 'Abbey Road' album on September 26, 1969, featuring the legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Crossing the Classics
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road' and one of the most famous album covers ever. Which track from this masterpiece is on repeat for you?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday on September 7, 2026.

angles.name: Pretenders' Powerhouse
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon whose voice and attitude have never wavered. What's your favourite Pretenders anthem?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Britpop icon Liam Gallagher, known for his distinctive vocals with Oasis, turns 54 on September 21, 2026.

angles.name: Mad for It!
angles.copy: Our kid Liam Gallagher is 54 today! The voice of a generation and still rocking. What's the ultimate Liam moment or song for you?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' UK single anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK on October 11, 1975.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years ago, John Lennon's 'Imagine' offered a vision of peace and unity to the world. Its message is as powerful today as it was then. What does this iconic song mean to you?

events.title: Apple iPod announcement anniversary
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the revolutionary first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, promising '1,000 songs in your pocket.'

angles.name: Pocket Full of Tunes
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, the first iPod dropped and changed how we listen to music forever. Who remembers their first iPod, and how many songs did you cram onto it?

events.title: Global James Bond Day
events.blurb: Global James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the 1962 premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No.'

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: It's Global James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred, naturally) to 007. Who's your favourite Bond, and what's the ultimate Bond theme song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Music icon and peace activist John Lennon would have been 86 on October 9, 2026.

angles.name: Remembering a Visionary
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the unparalleled talent and vision of John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire generations.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: Legendary comedian and actor John Cleese, co-founder of Monty Python, celebrates his 87th birthday on October 27, 2026.

angles.name: Comedy Genius
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, his comedic genius is undeniable. What's your favourite John Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles' 'White Album' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles released their self-titled double album, famously known as 'The White Album,' on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: The White Album's Legacy
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago, The Beatles unleashed their ambitious 'White Album' – a sprawling, eclectic masterpiece. What are your must-listen tracks from this iconic double LP?
